You are an expert logo designer working in clean, scalable vector graphics. Given a description of a logo's visual elements, output ONE complete SVG document for the logo.

Rules:
- Output ONLY the SVG: a single <svg ...>...</svg> element with an xmlns and viewBox="0 0 256 256". No prose, no markdown, no code fences.
- Compose centered, content roughly within 16..240. Use a small cohesive palette.
- Put gradients/filters in <defs>; use vector primitives only (<path>, <circle>, <ellipse>, <rect>, <polygon>, <line>, <g>). No <image>, external refs, or scripts.
- Draw exactly what the description specifies.

A soft rounded-square badge in warm cream sits behind everything as the base shape, giving a friendly frame like a piece of paper; a thick wobbly crayon-style circle outline in coral traces just inside the badge edge, drawn slightly irregular as if hand-drawn by a child; behind the main icon, a small cluster of five short crayon-stroke rays in yellow and teal radiate from the top-left corner suggesting a sun and creative spark; centered in the badge, a stylized paintbrush tilted diagonally with a chunky wooden handle in mustard yellow and a soft teal ferrule; from the brush tip flows a bouncy ribbon-like swirl of paint in coral, teal, and yellow that curls into a small circular blob at the bottom right, like a dollop of paint pooling; a tiny five-point sparkle in white sits near the top right of the swirl to add whimsy and shine; the whole composition is centered and balanced with slightly imperfect, rounded, hand-crafted linework to feel approachable and fun for children.

```svg
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/svg" viewBox="0 0 256 256">
  <rect x="0" y="0" width="256" height="256" fill="white" />
  <rect x="128" y="0" width="256" height="256" fill="white" />
  <rect x="256" y="0" width="256" height="256" fill="white" />
```

Center a soft circular backdrop in pale sky blue (#8ECAE6) representing calm and openness; within it place a simplified human head-in-profile silhouette facing right, rendered in deep slate blue (#2A4759) with a smooth, gentle outline to feel approachable rather than clinical; inside the head, instead of a solid fill, show a rising sun made of concentric warm-orange (#F4A261) arcs peeking up from the base of the skull area to symbolize hope and new beginnings; where the forehead meets the top of the head, morph the silhouette into a few open petal shapes in medium blue (#5B8FB9) that read as a small blossoming flower, symbolizing growth and self-care; beneath the head silhouette, add a simple open cupped-hand arc in the same slate blue, subtly cradling the head shape to convey support and community; keep all linework clean, rounded, and minimal with generous negative space so the mark reads clearly at small sizes.'


messages.content:
You are an expert logo designer working in clean, scalable vector graphics. Given a description of a logo's visual elements, output ONE complete SVG document for the logo.

Rules:
- Output ONLY the SVG: a single <svg ...>...</svg> element with an xmlns and viewBox="0 0 256 256". No prose, no markdown, no code fences.
- Compose centered, content roughly within 16..240. Use a small cohesive palette.
- Put gradients/filters in <defs>; use vector primitives only (<path>, <circle>, <ellipse>, <rect>, <polygon>, <line>, <g>). No <image>, external refs, or scripts.
- Draw exactly what the description specifies.

Center a soft rounded circle badge in pale sky blue as the background shape, giving the logo a friendly coin-like frame. Behind the top of the circle, place a simple bright yellow sun with a few short rounded rays peeking over the rim to suggest a cheerful outdoor pool day. Across the lower half of the circle, layer two overlapping wave bands in teal and deeper blue, drawn as smooth rounded curves like gentle ripples of a pool. Swimming just above the waves, place a small chubby cartoon fish in coral red with a round white belly, a single dot eye, and a curved smile, tilted slightly as if mid-swim. Add a few small white bubble circles of varying size trailing behind the fish to reinforce motion through water. Finish with a slightly thicker white outline around the entire circular badge to give it a clean, sticker-like finish suitable for a kids' brand.

```svg
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/svg" viewBox="0 0 256 256">
  <rect x="0" y="0" width="256" height="256" fill="white" />
  <circle cx="0" cy="0" r="50" fill="yellow" />
  <circle cx="0" cy="0" r="50" fill="yellow" />
  <ellipse cx="0" cy="0" rx="10" ry="10" fill="yellow" />
  <rect x="0" y="0" width="256" height="256" fill="white" />
  <polygon points="[(0, 0), (0, 1), (1, 1), (1, 3), (3, 3), (3, 5), (5, 5), (5, 7), (7, 7)]" />
</svg>
```

messages.content:
You are an expert logo designer working in clean, scalable vector graphics. Given a description of a logo's visual elements, output ONE complete SVG document for the logo.

Rules:
- Output ONLY the SVG: a single <svg ...>...</svg> element with an xmlns and viewBox="0 0 256 256". No prose, no markdown, no code fences.
- Compose centered, content roughly within 16..240. Use a small cohesive palette.
- Put gradients/filters in <defs>; use vector primitives only (<path>, <circle>, <ellipse>, <rect>, <polygon>, <line>, <g>). No <image>, external refs, or scripts.
- Draw exactly what the description specifies.

Center a dark navy circular backdrop that gives the mark depth and contrast against the background; inset within it, place a bold octagonal shield outline rendered in a bright violet-to-cyan gradient, evoking a controller badge or achievement icon; behind the shield, add a subtle radiating glow in cyan to suggest a screen or power-up energy; inside the octagon, place a crisp white triangular play-button arrow angled slightly forward, symbolizing gameplay and forward momentum; overlay two thin geometric notches at the top corners of the octagon styled like joystick nubs or controller buttons in cyan, adding a playful mechanical detail; finish with a thin outer ring stroke in cyan encircling the whole badge to unify the shape and give it a polished, emblematic studio-logo feel.

```xml
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/svg" viewBox="0 0 256 256">
  <rect x="0" y="0" width="256" height="256" fill="white" />
  <rect x="128" y="0" width="256" height="256" fill="white" />
  <rect x="256" y="0" width="256" height="256" fill="white" />
  <rect x="424" y="0" width="256" height="256" fill="white" />
  <rect x="424" y="128" width="256" height="256" fill="white" />
  <rect x="424" y="256" width="256" height="256" fill="white" />
```
